snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review binds the Reader transform body-free failure repair and responsive selection-toolbar geometry correction on sole main 2b2d5889. Unsupported internal error domains are normalized at the existing public failure boundary, unexpected transform submission failures return the strict failed projection, and the toolbar remeasures the selection and itself after Reader layout changes without changing action authority, copy, persistence or API behavior.

reviewers.task: Reader transform failure and responsive toolbar review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning did not author the Development or UI commits and independently compared their exact service, renderer and test bytes with the published Reader selection contracts, shared error taxonomy and existing focus owners on sole main 2b2d5889.

dimensions.summary: ReaderSelectionActionService remains the transform owner and App retains the established toolbar and Reader focus owners; no duplicate action, navigation or error-taxonomy owner is added.

The published Reader selection contract remains unchanged: internal errors are projected through an allowed body-free public domain, and responsive geometry remeasurement changes no selection identity, transform authority or proposal lifecycle.
Existing requirement, build, exit and phase statuses remain unchanged; the candidate closes two bounded Reader defects and does not promote broader Reader mutation or release completion.

Focused error-schema, Home Agent, Reader action and renderer tests cover normalization, strict failed projection, responsive remeasurement, keyboard behavior and focus restoration; typecheck, build and UI visual gates remain directly runnable.

The product delta remains confined to two existing services, App.tsx and focused regressions; this excluded recipe records the independent publication review without adding or rewriting an owner document.

verification.command: Independent review of Reader transform failure and toolbar geometry on sole main 2b2d5889
verification.evidence: Review confirms unsupported internal domains cannot cross the public failure schema, unexpected transform failures return one strict body-free result, and 420, 840 and 1240 Reader layouts reposition the existing toolbar without changing action or focus ownership.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decisions and locked claims pass with no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green; focused service and UI tests, typecheck, build, boundary and visual-contract verification bind the exact repair while the union recipe retains the published Reader, Update, release and security roots.

coordination.taskTitle: Reader transform failure and responsive toolbar repair
coordination.compatibility: The candidate changes only error normalization, strict transform failure projection and responsive toolbar geometry; it adds no API, persistence, Job, permission, locale or mutation authority.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning reviewed the exact Development and UI bytes on sole main 2b2d5889 and records these bounded Reader repairs without restyling the toolbar or claiming broader feature completion.

nonBlockingRisks: A model/provider failure may still prevent a transform from producing an applied result; this candidate guarantees only the strict body-free failure contract and does not claim provider availability.
No Reader mutation, proposal, Activity, Undo, Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase status is promoted by these repairs.
Real macOS evidence covers responsive Reader geometry at 420, 840 and 1240; this candidate makes no additional Windows geometry claim.
